channel.title: Çağatay Özcan Jagiello Gutt - Thoughts & Updates
channel.description: Cognitive Science Student (M.Sc.) & Molecular Biologist (B.Sc.) | Artificial Consciousness, BCI & HMI Research | Physiological Computing & AI

item.title: Project Update: AnalysisToolbox
item.author: Unknown
item.description:
Recent activity in AnalysisToolbox:

Recent Commits

  • ece4676 refactor: move scaffold creation before bootstrap push
  • 7927283 feat: bootstrap push of all changes before analysis starts
  • 91ea267 feat: sync L2 folder with chunked push on each participant finalization
  • b7f6d4b feat: chunked git push for L2 finalization
  • ee60669 feat: chunked git push to stay under GitHub 2 GB pack limit

This project is actively maintained as part of my commitment to open and transparent science.

View on GitHub → | See all projects →

item.title: Website Update
item.author: Unknown
item.description:
This website has been updated with the following changes:

Recent Changes

  • 52268d4 debug: add log parsing diagnostics and line count indicator
  • 3971a41 fix: detect LFS pointer stubs and show clear diagnostic message
  • dac4a41 fix: log viewer scrollability - flexbox layout + scroll reset
  • 58175a1 remove: strip all LFS detection code - not needed for small parquet files
  • cfca63a fix: detect Git LFS pointers, use marked.js for markdown, scroll log to top

The site continues to sync automatically with GitHub (projects) and ORCID (publications).

View source on GitHub →

item.title: Welcome to My Research Hub
item.author: Unknown
item.description:
This website serves as my central scientific presence, automatically syncing with GitHub (active projects), ORCID (publications), and other platforms.

What to Expect

This blog will feature:

  • Research updates — Progress on neural-autonomic synchrony and consciousness research
  • Volt engagement — Data analytics and digital policy insights
  • Technical insights — Tools, methods, and open science practices
  • CCC reflections — Digital rights and technological transparency

Philosophy

Everything here operates on principles of open and transparent science. Code, data, and ideas are shared to advance collaborative knowledge.

To add new posts, simply create a markdown file in content/blog/ with this format and commit to GitHub.
